questions.questionText:
O is the centre of a circle. Points A, B and C are on the circumference.
Angle AOC = 130°.
Find angle ABC, giving a reason.

markScheme.desc: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference (same arc AC)
Reason stated: angle at centre is twice angle at circumference

workingSteps: The angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc.
questions.explanation: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference (same arc).

questions.questionText:
A, B, C and D are points on a circle.
Angle ABD = 35° and angle DBC = 40°.
Find angle ACD, giving reasons.

markScheme.desc: Angles in the same segment are equal
Angle ACD = angle ABD (same arc AD)
Angles in the same segment are equal oe

workingSteps: ACD and ABD are both inscribed angles subtended by arc AD
Angles in the same segment are equal
questions.explanation: Angles in the same segment (subtended by the same arc) are equal.

questions.context: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. Angle DAB = (2x + 10)°, angle BCD = (3x − 20)°, angle ABC = 95°.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Find the value of x, giving a reason.

markScheme.desc: Opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°

workingSteps: DAB + BCD = 180° (opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral)

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Find angle DAB and angle BCD.

parts.label: c

markScheme.desc: Opposite angles sum to 180°: ADC + ABC = 180°

workingSteps: ADC + ABC = 180° (opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral)
